overview: Port Security restricts which devices may use a switch port, based on source MAC address, protecting against unauthorized devices and basic MAC-flooding style attacks.
definitions: Sticky learning: dynamically learned MAC addresses are automatically added into the running-config.
Maximum: the number of MAC addresses allowed on a secured port (default 1).
err-disabled: the state a port enters after a Shutdown-mode violation; it stays down until manually recovered.
core_concepts: Violation modes: Shutdown (default — port goes err-disabled, logs sent), Restrict (drops offending traffic, increments counter, sends SNMP/Syslog), Protect (drops offending traffic silently, no logging).
Sticky MAC addresses are only persisted if the running-config is saved; otherwise they are lost on reload.
protocol_operation: Switch learns the first MAC(s) seen on the port up to the configured maximum.
Any additional, unrecognized MAC triggers the configured violation action.
packet_flow: Frame arrives on a secured port -> switch checks source MAC against the port's secured MAC table -> known/under max: forwarded normally -> new MAC beyond max: violation action taken per configured mode.
default_values: Maximum secure MAC addresses per port: 1.

config_commands.purpose: Enable port security on the interface.
Set the maximum number of secure MAC addresses.
Enable sticky learning of MAC addresses into the running-config.

verification_commands.purpose: Show violation mode, max, count, and last violating MAC for a port.
Show the table of secured MAC addresses.

show_commands.purpose: Primary verification command for port security state.

cli_examples.title: Recovering an err-disabled port
cli_examples.explanation: Required specifically when violation mode is Shutdown — Restrict/Protect never take the port down, so recovery is unnecessary in those modes.

config_examples.title: Typical access-port lockdown
config_examples.code:
interface GigabitEthernet0/5
 switchport mode access
 switchport port-security
 switchport port-security maximum 1
 switchport port-security mac-address sticky
 switchport port-security violation shutdown

troubleshooting.symptom: Interface is err-disabled; show port-security interface shows Violation Mode = Shutdown, Violation Count = 1.
troubleshooting.cause: An unauthorized second device (often a small unmanaged switch) was connected, exceeding the configured maximum.
troubleshooting.fix: Identify and remove the extra device, then 'shutdown' / 'no shutdown' the interface to recover — required only because the mode is Shutdown.

best_practices: Shut down all unused switch ports and place them in a dummy/unused VLAN.
Save the running-config after sticky MAC addresses are learned, or they will be lost on reboot.
security_notes: Port Security defends the access layer against unauthorized device connections and basic MAC-flooding, but is not a substitute for 802.1X-style authentication.
interview_questions: Explain the difference between Restrict and Protect violation modes.
Why must you manually recover a port after a Shutdown-mode violation?
What happens to sticky-learned MACs if the switch reloads without a 'write'?
exam_traps: Forgetting to save the running-config after sticky learning — addresses are lost on reboot.
Assuming any violation mode auto-recovers the port — only errdisable recovery features (not covered here) or manual shut/no-shut do.
enterprise_notes: Commonly deployed as a baseline hardening control on every wired access port in enterprise switching designs.
